questions.question: What are command line arguments in C++?
How are command line arguments passed to the main function in C++?
keyPoints: Use of argc and argv variables
Syntax for passing command line arguments

questions.question: What is the purpose of the argc variable in the main function?
What is the purpose of the argv array in the main function?
How do you access the command line arguments in the main function?
keyPoints: Syntax for accessing command line arguments

questions.question: Write a C++ program that takes two command line arguments: a string and an integer. The program should print the string and the integer to the console.
keyPoints: Use of argc and argv variables
Syntax for passing command line arguments

questions.question: Write a C++ program that takes a variable number of command line arguments. The program should print each argument to the console.
keyPoints: Use of argc and argv variables
Syntax for passing command line arguments
Use of loop to iterate over arguments

questions.question: Write a C++ program that uses the getopt function to parse command line arguments. The program should take two options: -h and -v. The -h option should print a help message, and the -v option should print the version number of the program.
keyPoints: Syntax for parsing command line arguments
Use of switch statement to handle options
